statute: Mostly local municipal licensing. Title 32, chapter 73 (electrical); Title 32, chapter 17 (plumbing). Written contract required by custom for projects over $3,000.
license_notes: Maine licensing is primarily local. Electrical and plumbing contractors require state licenses. Written contract required (by industry practice and local ordinances) for projects over $3,000.

mandatory_clauses.label: Contractor information
Written contract requirement
mandatory_clauses.text: Under Maine law and industry practice, this written contract is provided for any project valued at $3,000 or more.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Local penalties. Electrical/plumbing unlicensed = state penalties. No license required for Maine lien rights.
notes: Maine licensing is primarily local. State licenses required for electricians and plumbers. Written contracts are standard practice for jobs over $3,000. No license required to exercise lien rights. Check local municipality for GC licensing requirements.
